title: e2e: cover WebSocket reconnect within 30s grace period
description: The client has WS auto-reconnect with a 30-second grace period (CLAUDE.md). No e2e test exercises this path. Add a test that simulates a WS disconnect mid-game, reconnects within the grace window using the same token, and verifies the player's game state is intact. The night-summary-reconnect spec (PR #45) covers the UI modal; this is about game-state integrity on reconnect.

title: e2e: cover non-host player leaving mid-game
description: host-leaves.spec.ts covers the host leaving, but no test covers a non-host player leaving during an active game. Add a test that starts a 4-player game, has one joiner call POST /leave mid-game, and asserts the remaining players' state reflects the departure (player removed from list, game can continue to next phase).

title: e2e: cover day vote tie (no elimination)
description: CLAUDE.md documents: day vote tie → no elimination (Davidoff rules). No e2e test verifies this behavior. Add a test that sets up a day phase, submits equal votes for two different players via IPC, force-resolves, and asserts no player is eliminated and the game advances to the next night.

title: e2e: cover mafia win condition
description: All complete-game tests reach game-over but no test asserts a mafia victory specifically. With 4 players (1 mafia, 3 town) mafia wins when they equal or outnumber non-mafia — after one kill with no day elimination. Add a test that verifies the mafia win condition fires and the game-over banner reads 'Mafia wins!'.

title: e2e: cover night role mechanics (kill, doctor save, sheriff investigate)
description: No e2e test exercises the actual night role actions. All complete-game and game-round specs force-resolve every night, so the mafia kill, doctor save, and sheriff investigate flows are never exercised end-to-end. Add tests that submit real night actions via window.mafia IPC and assert the correct outcomes (player eliminated, save preventing elimination, sheriff result returned).
